tasks_completed.description: PATCH-1.a: Restored LINE_LIMIT from 1000 to 600 in pretool-read-size-guard.py (AC5 / M3)
tasks_completed.changes: Line 17: `LINE_LIMIT = 1000` -> `LINE_LIMIT = 600`. Aligns code with module docstring (line 5: '600 lines') and CLAUDE.md whitelist (line 46: 'Read (<=600 lines, ...)'). Reverts the L3 drift introduced by commit 9204c25 (May 7).
tasks_completed.rationale: Per ticket §Acceptance Criteria AC5 and Finding-3: code/docstring/CLAUDE.md must all say 600. The user-facing contract is the CLAUDE.md whitelist, which says 600.

tasks_completed.description: PATCH-1.b: Added pathlib + lib.allowlist imports and has_consent() helper to pretool-read-size-guard.py (AC1, AC2)
tasks_completed.changes: Added `from pathlib import Path` plus `sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))` + `from lib.allowlist import read_grant` after the stdlib import block. Added module-level `has_consent(session_id)` helper that mirrors pretool-orchestrator-gate.py:66-71 verbatim — flag.exists() AND content.strip() == 'true', try/except wrapped, fail-False.
tasks_completed.rationale: Required wiring for AC1 (/do bypass) and AC2 (/allow bypass). Helper signature/semantics copied byte-for-byte from canonical reference per dispatch constraint.

tasks_completed.description: PATCH-1.c: Inserted /do + /allow bypass branch in main() of pretool-read-size-guard.py (AC1, AC2)
tasks_completed.changes: After `if is_exempt(data, file_path): sys.exit(0)` (line 66-67) and before `line_count = count_lines(file_path)` (line 77), inserted: `if data.get('tool_name') == 'Read' and not data.get('agent_id'): sid = data.get('session_id') or os.environ.get('CLAUDE_SESSION_ID', 'default'); try: if has_consent(sid) or read_grant('Read', sid): sys.exit(0); except Exception: pass`. is_exempt() body unchanged. Block message unchanged.
tasks_completed.rationale: Placement matches OBJ-4 resolution (AFTER is_exempt, BEFORE count_lines). Defensive `not data.get('agent_id')` guard keeps the body inert for subagents even if is_exempt is ever reordered. Fail-open on exception per dispatch constraint.

tasks_completed.description: PATCH-2.a: Converted pretool-block-enterworktree.sh from CLAUDE_TOOL_NAME env reader to stdin JSON reader (AC6, AC7, AC8 prerequisite)
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced `TOOL_NAME="${CLAUDE_TOOL_NAME:-}"` with `INPUT=$(cat)` + python3 inline tool_name extraction, falling open on JSON parse failure (2>/dev/null + empty-string default). Tool-name short-circuit (exit 0 on non-EnterWorktree) preserved. Pattern copied from pretool-write-guard.sh:6-15 idiom.
tasks_completed.rationale: Required to support /do flag + /allow grant reads (need session_id from JSON payload). Risk 3 documented in ticket: every PreToolUse hook in the directory reads stdin JSON; the env var was a Claude Code convenience, not a primary contract.

tasks_completed.description: PATCH-2.b: Inserted main-agent-only /do + /allow bypass block in pretool-block-enterworktree.sh (AC6, AC7)
tasks_completed.changes: Added agent_id check, sid derivation (data.session_id || env CLAUDE_SESSION_ID || 'default'), `/tmp/claude-orchestrator-consent-<sid>.flag` content-equality check (cat == 'true' on exit 0), and inline-python read_grant('EnterWorktree', sid) bridge. Gated by `if [ "$_BEW_IS_SUB" != "1" ]` so subagents stay unconditionally blocked (per ticket Risk 4 / OBJ-3). All variables prefixed with _BEW_ to avoid namespace collision. HOOKS_DIR derived via `cd dirname BASH_SOURCE && pwd`.
tasks_completed.rationale: Risk 4 (codex feedback #5): subagents are NOT exempted by this patch. Pattern copied from pretool-write-guard.sh:17-46 idiom exactly. Block message at lines 58-66 preserved verbatim (AC8).

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: Original gap: never added to the hook from inception. Closely-related commits: 150efdb (Apr 16 — LINE_LIMIT 200->600) and 9204c25 (May 7 — LINE_LIMIT 600->1000 + CHUNK_LIMIT carve-out, introduced docstring/CLAUDE.md drift).
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Both prior attempts (150efdb, 9204c25) were L3 threshold tweaks that treated the symptom (file-too-large block) without addressing the bypass-coverage gap. The /do/allow framework existed only in pretool-orchestrator-gate.py; subsequent hooks were not retrofitted. pretool-block-enterworktree.sh predates the /do contract being formalized in CLAUDE.md.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Adds L4 logic branches that actually wire up the /do (consent flag content == 'true') and /allow (read_grant) contract in both hooks, mirroring the canonical idiom from 8 already-honoring sibling hooks. Also reverts the L3 drift introduced by attempt 2 (LINE_LIMIT 1000 -> 600) to align code with docstring + CLAUDE.md.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: PATCH-2 mandates converting from env-var-only to stdin-JSON reader (~25 lines, canonical idiom from pretool-write-guard.sh) PLUS bypass branch (~30 lines, canonical idiom). Both transformations are byte-for-byte from BA-authorized canonical templates and cannot be compressed without diverging from the canonical idiom. PATCH-1 is 22 lines (just under the 20-line threshold's spirit — 5 imports/helper + 6-line bypass branch + 1 LINE_LIMIT char + 10 lines of whitespace/blank-lines). All additions are on the approved surface (files_to_modify); no scope expansion.

imported_by: INDEX.md (top-level documentation index, no behavior coupling)
blast_radius.note: No code-level imports of the hook from other Python modules. Settings.json registers it for PreToolUse globally; the registration shape is unchanged. Behavioral contract preserved: same stdin JSON input shape, same exit codes for the unchanged paths (AC3, AC4 verified), new exit-0 paths for the documented bypass (AC1, AC2 verified).

No code-level callers. The env-var-only contract (CLAUDE_TOOL_NAME) replaced with stdin-JSON; canonical PreToolUse hook protocol per Claude Code (Risk 3 documented in ticket). Behavioral contract preserved: same exit codes / messages for the unchanged paths (AC8 verified). New exit-0 paths added for documented bypass (AC6, AC7 verified).

dev.qa_notes: Smoke tests already verified all 9 ACs end-to-end with crafted stdin payloads (see self_verification.smoke_check). QA can re-execute the same smoke harness; suggested sid pattern: 'qa-<task-id>'. AC5 verification is 3-way: grep LINE_LIMIT in pretool-read-size-guard.py (line 21), grep 'larger than 600 lines' in the same file's docstring (line 5), grep 'Read (.le.600 lines' in /root/.claude/CLAUDE.md (line 46). AC9 verification: `git diff HEAD -- hooks/pretool-orchestrator-gate.py hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh hooks/pretool-subagent-enforce.py hooks/pretool-aggregate-check.py hooks/prompt-workflow.py hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh hooks/stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh` returns empty.

self_verification.notes: python3 -m py_compile on pretool-read-size-guard.py: OK. bash -n on pretool-block-enterworktree.sh: OK. AC1 (Read+/do flag): exit 0. AC2 (Read+/allow exact-pattern grant {'pattern':'Read','is_regex':false}): exit 0 with '[ALLOW] grant matched for Read, consume deferred to PostToolUse' on stderr (read_grant did not delete grant). AC3 (subagent Read agent_id, no /do, no /allow, 700-line file): exit 0. AC4 (plain main-agent Read 700-line file, no /do, no /allow): exit 2 with verbatim '[Read Size Guard] File too large' message and limit/CHUNK_LIMIT both shown as 600. AC5: grep LINE_LIMIT shows '21:LINE_LIMIT = 600'; sed 5p shows 'Blocks the main agent from reading files larger than 600 lines.'; CLAUDE.md line 46 still reads 'Read (<=600 lines, capped by ~/.claude/hooks/pretool-read-size-guard.py)'. AC6 (EnterWorktree+/do): exit 0. AC7 (EnterWorktree+/allow exact pattern): exit 0 with '[ALLOW] grant matched for EnterWorktree' on stderr. AC8 (plain main-agent EnterWorktree): exit 2 with verbatim 'BLOCKED: EnterWorktree uses origin/main as start point, not local HEAD.' and create-worktree.sh recommendation. Extra: subagent EnterWorktree (agent_id present) STILL exits 2 with same block message — confirms Risk 4 / OBJ-3 (subagents unconditionally blocked). Extra: malformed JSON on stdin → exit 0 (fail-open per constraint). AC9: git diff returns 0 changes on each of the 9 already-honoring files.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 3 findings. (1) EnterWorktree subagent block could be bypassable in non-agent_id subagent contexts (subagent_id / agent_path / CLAUDE_AGENT_ID env). (2) Read-size guard exempts only agent_id, not the broader lib.subagent.is_subagent_context aliases. (3) The .claude/.hook-refactor-allow sentinel was created during this cycle and left in place; codex flagged it as a /close-debate risk. Smoke tests codex ran confirmed all 9 ACs pass and confirmed subagent-EnterWorktree-with-do exits 0 (which codex flagged as Finding-1).
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Findings 1 and 2 classified out_of_scope per Calling Protocol Rule 3 (caller filters): BA spec explicitly bound both hooks to data.get('agent_id') semantics (AC3 verbatim, ticket Technical Hints PATCH-1+PATCH-2 sketches, dispatch constraint 'Subagent agent_id exemption at pretool-read-size-guard.py:21-25 stays unchanged'). Importing lib.subagent.is_subagent_context would expand the surface beyond BA-authorized scope and would violate pre_existing_guards (agent_id check, removal_authorized: false). The user-stated requirement is honored by the current implementation; codex's broader-subagent-alias coverage is a separate ticket. Finding 3 surfaced under blocking_issues for orchestrator/user attention: cannot self-fix because pretool-bash-safety.sh blocks `rm` and Subagent Hook Discipline forbids circumvention; documented as REQUEST in blocking_issues. Decision rationale: Rule 4 (when in doubt, the requirement wins) — user requirement was /do+/allow bypass for the named surface; that is delivered.

codex_observation_only: Codex Finding-1: EnterWorktree subagent block uses agent_id-only detection; subagents identified via subagent_id/agent_path/CLAUDE_AGENT_ID env (per hooks/lib/subagent.py) could bypass via /do or /allow. Out-of-scope per BA Risk 4 + dispatch constraint binding to data.get('agent_id'). Separate ticket if user wants lib.subagent-grade detection extended to this surface.

Codex Finding-2: Read-size-guard is_exempt() exempts only data.get('agent_id'); subagent contexts using subagent_id/agent_path/parent_agent_id/env markers would still be blocked on >600-line Reads. Explicitly forbidden by dispatch constraint 'Subagent agent_id exemption at pretool-read-size-guard.py:21-25 stays unchanged (AC3 / M6 no-regression)' AND by pre_existing_guards entry with removal_authorized: false. The current behavior matches existing AC3 verbatim. If broader subagent-class detection is later desired, BA must re-spec.

blocking_issues.issue: Sentinel file .claude/.hook-refactor-allow created at /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/.claude/.hook-refactor-allow remains on disk
blocking_issues.context: pretool-claude-config-guard.py required the sentinel to permit Edits to hook files (spec-20260424-233926 §5.2.4 R4.2). Created at the start of this cycle to enable the two authorized edits.
blocking_issues.why_unfixable_by_dev: pretool-bash-safety.sh blocks `rm` for the dev subagent; Subagent Hook Discipline forbids circumventing rejected commands. The Edit tool cannot delete files. The agent file's Subagent Hook Discipline (CLAUDE.md global) instructs dev subagents to PAUSE + report rather than circumvent.
blocking_issues.request_to_orchestrator: Please delete /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/.claude/.hook-refactor-allow once QA has finished validating, before /close. The sentinel disables claude-config-guard's hook-protection invariant and should not persist across sessions.

recommendations: Orchestrator: after QA pass, delete .claude/.hook-refactor-allow to restore claude-config-guard's hook-protection invariant (see blocking_issues[0]).

Out-of-scope-but-noteworthy (codex findings 1 + 2, BA out_of_scope_observations cross-ref): if the user later wants subagent-class detection to also cover subagent_id/agent_path/CLAUDE_AGENT_ID aliases on these two surfaces, that requires a separate ticket — currently BA scope is bound to data.get('agent_id') verbatim per AC3 + pre_existing_guards.

Out-of-scope-but-noteworthy (BA out_of_scope_observations cross-ref): lib/allowlist.read_grant uses exact-or-substr semantics at PreToolUse while posttool consume_grant_for_posttool branch 3 uses exact-only. This asymmetry is pre-existing (8 other call sites unchanged). AC2 and AC7 use exact-match grant bodies to avoid exposing the asymmetry. A future ticket could tighten read_grant to exact-only or widen posttool to exact-or-substr — either direction closes the gap.

Out-of-scope-but-noteworthy (BA out_of_scope_observations cross-ref): pretool-workflow-gate.py blocks every non-TodoWrite/TodoRead tool while a workflow bookmark is unacknowledged; it has no subagent exemption and no /do bypass. CLAUDE.md does not currently document /do as bypassing workflow checklists. If the user later wants /do to bypass workflow gating too, that requires a separate ticket and a contract clarification in CLAUDE.md.
